priority_order: 128-column tile, 8 columns per thread, shared memory staging for A and Z tiles, vectorized float4 loads/stores, unrolled rank-16 FMAs
variations: 64-column tile, 4 columns per thread; 256-column tile, 16 columns per thread; adjust rows per block (e.g., 4, 8, 16) to balance occupancy and register pressure
shape-aware dispatch if a single tile configuration underperforms across d in [3584, 4608]
llm_advice.rationale: Baseline ATen reference shows no speedup due to multiple GEMM and add launches. The recommended path is to keep cuBLAS for W @ X and B.T @ X, then fuse Y += A @ Z into a single custom kernel exploiting the fixed rank-16. Starting with a 128-column tile, 8 columns per thread, shared memory staging, and vectorized float4 operations is a high-priority baseline that balances occupancy and register usage. Subsequent variations will explore tile sizes and rows per block to find the best configuration across the runtime shape range.
risk_controls: correctness gate (max_abs_err=0, rel_l2_err=0) across all d
static review for safety and adherence to constraints
benchmark against baseline across all d before promotion
stop_conditions: after exploring 3 distinct tile configurations without improvement, fallback to best so far
if any candidate fails correctness, discard and revert to previous best

notes: Use ATen/cuBLAS for the d x d GEMM.
Explore kernels that fuse Y += A @ T for rank 16.
